title: Summer Solstice Gathering
description:
Join us for an evening of music, food, and conversation as we celebrate the longest day of the year.

## What to expect

- Live acoustic music from local artists
- Seasonal food and drinks
- Outdoor seating under string lights
- A bonfire at dusk

Bring a blanket and your favourite summer story.
author: stadius-admin

title: Independent Film Screening: The Quiet City
description:
A one-night screening of the award-winning documentary *The Quiet City*, followed by a Q&A with the director.

## About the film

Shot over three years, this film explores the hidden corners of urban life — the people, the stories, and the spaces we overlook.

## Details

Doors open at 19:00. Screening begins at 19:30. The Q&A runs until approximately 21:30. Popcorn and drinks provided.
author: editor1

title: Neighbourhood Street Festival
description:
Our annual street festival returns with food stalls, craft markets, live performances, and activities for all ages.

## Highlights

- 30+ food and craft vendors
- Kids' corner with face painting and puppet shows
- Two live music stages
- Evening dance floor

The street will be closed to traffic from 10:00 to 22:00. Free entry for all.
author: stadius-admin

title: Poetry Night Under the Stars
description:
An open-mic poetry night in the community garden. Bring your own work or a favourite piece by someone else to share.

## The vibe

Candlelit tables, quiet applause, and the sound of wind through the trees. All styles welcome — from sonnets to spoken word.

Tea and biscuits provided. This is a sober, all-ages event.
author: editor1

title: Weekend Book Swap
description:
Bring up to five books you have loved and swap them for something new. Fiction, non-fiction, poetry, graphic novels — all genres welcome.

## How it works

1. Drop off your books at the check-in table
2. Browse the collection
3. Take home as many as you brought

Leftover books are donated to the public library. Coffee and pastries available.
author: stadius-admin

title: Late Summer Jazz Picnic
description:
A relaxed afternoon of live jazz in the park. Bring a picnic, a blanket, and someone you like.

## Line-up

- 14:00 — The Riverside Trio
- 15:30 — Ella & The Afternoon Band
- 17:00 — Late Set with the Monday Quartet

Food trucks on site. No glass bottles please.
author: editor1

title: Autumn Equinox Potluck
description:
Celebrate the turning of the season with a community potluck dinner. Everyone brings a dish to share.

## What to bring

- A savoury or sweet dish (label allergens please)
- Your own plate and cutlery (we are reducing waste)
- A candle for the communal table

We will set up long tables in the hall. All ages welcome.
author: stadius-admin

title: Printmaking Workshop
description:
Learn the basics of linocut printmaking in this hands-on workshop. You will carve your own block, ink it, and take home a small edition of prints.

## What is provided

- All materials (linoleum blocks, carving tools, ink, paper)
- Instruction from a working printmaker
- Tea and biscuits

No experience necessary. Limited to 12 participants.
author: editor1

title: Harvest Market & Cider Tasting
description:
A celebration of the autumn harvest with local producers, fresh produce, and a curated cider tasting.

## At the market

- Seasonal fruit and vegetables from regional farms
- Artisan bread, cheese, honey, and preserves
- Cider tasting flights (alcoholic and non-alcoholic)
- Live folk music

Entry is free. Cider tasting tickets are available at the door.
author: stadius-admin

title: Board Game Night
description:
An evening of board games, card games, and friendly competition. Bring your own favourites or pick from our library of over 100 games.

## The details

- Games for all skill levels available
- Staff on hand to teach unfamiliar games
- Hot food and drinks served until 22:00
- Teams and solo players welcome

Prizes for the last table standing.
author: editor1

title: Winter Lantern Parade
description:
As the nights draw in, we gather with handmade lanterns and walk through the city together. A quiet, beautiful tradition.

## How to join

- Lantern-making workshops run the week before the parade
- Bring your lantern to the starting point by 17:30
- The parade sets off at dusk and walks a 2 km route

Hot chocolate and mulled cider served at the end point. All ages welcome.
location: Starting at City Hall steps, ending at Riverside Park
author: stadius-admin

title: End of Year Celebration
description:
Raise a glass to the year gone by. A warm, informal evening of food, drink, and looking back at what we have built together.

## The evening

- A short retrospective of Stadius highlights from the year
- Dinner served family-style
- Dancing until midnight

Dress warmly — there will be a fire pit outside for quiet conversations. Partners and friends welcome.
author: editor1
